title: chore: replace health check with simple dead app removal
author: voodoohop

gist.headline: Spring cleaning the app garden
gist.blurb: We've simplified how we keep the app directory fresh. Instead of a complex health scoring system, we now have a friendly weekly bot that gently prunes apps that have gone offline.
gist.summary: Replaced the complex daily app health check system with a simpler weekly workflow that identifies and removes dead links after multiple verification attempts.
gist.impact: Maintains a cleaner, more reliable app directory for the community by automatically pruning broken projects while reducing unnecessary CI noise.
gist.visual_concept: The PR is about removing dead apps/links and cleaning up the directory. Metaphor: Pruning dead leaves/plants from a garden. Characters: Polly the bee wearing a gardener's hat, and the Nomnom creature eating the dead leaves/code.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny gardener's hat, gently pruning withered brown code-plants from a lime green (#ecf874) flower box. The round tan nomnom creature sits nearby, happily eating the dried leaves. Soft pastel gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
